accurate.why: All claims accurately reflect the bill text; the description of the Advisory Council composition and Secretary's duties matches the statute.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; all specific details about agencies, deadlines, and membership requirements match the bill.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the purpose as establishing a National Parkinson's Project under HHS to prevent, diagnose, treat, and cure Parkinson's.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly notes the act amends the Public Health Service Act by adding a new Part W.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the 2035 sunset and key deadlines (24 months for first annual assessment, 18 months for first Advisory Council report).

effective_dates.why: Notes the 2035 sunset and specific deadlines (24 months, 18 months) for the Secretary's and Advisory Council's first required actions.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers the National Parkinson's Project establishment, Secretary's duties, Advisory Council composition and requirements, information sharing, and sunset provision.

coherent.why: Well-organized with logical progression through the bill's provisions.

concise.why: Extremely detailed — covers nearly every sub-provision; more granular than needed for a summary and borders on a restatement rather than a summary.

correct_entities.why: All agencies (CDC, NIH, CMS, FDA, VA, DoD, etc.) correctly named as specified in the bill.

correct_figures.why: All deadlines (24 months, 18 months, calendar year 2035) and approval date (July 2, 2024) correctly stated.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
